intent:
First day at school. The teacher writes the narrator's name; the friend behind doesn't know the narrator yet, has no pencil; the narrator shows their pencil to the friend. The pencil is the anchor — it appears in three sentences and changes who can see/use it (narrator's hand → shown to friend). Bootstrap slot 4 of 10 (Phase 4 reload, classroom seed). Mints exactly the 8 seed-aligned words (学校, 先生, 名前, 書く, 鉛筆, 後ろ, 友達, 知る) — drops 前 from the seed in favor of 知る so masen can be introduced verbally rather than just on existence. Introduces 2 new grammar points (G008_te_iru on 持っています; G036_masen on 知りません). Reinforces all three must-reinforce items from the brief: G013_mashita_past (s1 書きました, s5 見せました), G007_te_form (s2 持って), G035_arimasen (s4 ありません). Reinforces W00033 見せる from story 3.

PREMISE CONTRACT (story 4)
  one_sentence_event:    The narrator shows their pencil to the friend behind, after the teacher has written the narrator's name and the friend has revealed they have no pencil and don't know the narrator.
  change_of_state:       at s0 the narrator's name and pencil are unknown to the friend behind; at the closer the friend has seen the name (written by the teacher in s1) and been shown the pencil. friend's knowledge X≠Y.
  scene_novelty_claim:   I am NOT writing a {home_morning, kitchen, walk_to_shop} story because the last 3 were. Scene is classroom — first appearance in the v2 corpus.
  anchor_novelty_claim:  Anchor is 鉛筆 (pencil). It has never appeared in v2. Last 5 anchors were tea_cup, apple, egg.
  closer_shape_claim:    Closer shape: action verb 見せました (transfer-of-view). NOT a noun-pile, NOT 「Nは Adj です」, NOT 「Nは Yを Zに あります」 (s2/s3 mirrors). Distinct from the three banned closer shapes.
  why_not_filler:        私 — narrates, holds the pencil, performs the closing transfer. 先生 — writes the name (the act that makes the narrator legible to the friend). 友達 — is the recipient who lacks knowledge and a pencil; without them the closer transfer has no destination. 鉛筆 — physical anchor: held (s2), absent on the friend's side (s4), shown across the gap (s5).
  obligations_absorbed:  G013_mashita_past — s1 書きました (the teacher's writing) and s5 見せました (the closing transfer). G007_te_form — s2 持って. G035_arimasen — s4 友達の鉛筆はありません (the friend's lack motivates s5's transfer; not bolted on). G008_te_iru intro — s2 持っています (state of holding, naturally describing the narrator's still posture before the transfer). G036_masen intro — s3 知りません (the friend doesn't know me, justifying why the transfer matters).

FORBIDDEN THIS STORY:
  scene_class    (last 3 stories): home_morning, kitchen, walk_to_shop
  anchor_object  (last 5 stories): apple, egg, tea_cup
  closer_shape   (last 3 stories): noun-WA-noun, noun-WA-noun-NO-noun-NI-noun, noun-WA-noun-WO-noun
  opening_seqs   (last 3 stories' first 3 content tokens):
    - 朝 / 家 / 台所
    - 朝 / 母 / 私
    - 朝 / 台所 / 卵

All four zones are honored: scene_class=classroom, anchor=鉛筆, closer shape is noun-WA-noun-NI-noun-WO-verb-MASHITA, opening starts 私/は/学校 (no 朝).

Deferral note: seed_plan vocab includes 前 and the grammar_seed includes N5_te_imasu (G008) and N5_masen (G036) plus N5_ni_target. 前 is dropped to keep the mint count at the 8 cap; 後ろ alone covers the spatial seed (the friend is 後ろ). N5_ni_target is realized via the existing G004_ni_location tag on s5 (私は友達に鉛筆を見せました) — the catalog distinction between ni-location and ni-target collapses to G004 in the tagger today. No override consumed — all swaps are within seed neighborhood.

sentences.jp: 先生は私の名前を書きました。
私は鉛筆を持っています。
後ろの友達は私の名前を知りません。
sentences.en: The friend behind me doesn't know my name.

sentences.jp: 友達の鉛筆はありません。
私は友達に鉛筆を見せました。
sentences.en: I showed my pencil to the friend.
